prompt: A hyper-realistic, high-contrast documentary macro photograph of a delicate, translucent glass-blown sparrow. A heavy, blank, brutalist steel industrial caliper, which has absolutely no numerical markings, scales, or tick marks on its surface, is clamped tightly around the sparrow's torso. The metal jaws are visibly biting into the glass, causing a fine, jagged web of fractal cracks to radiate across the bird's body. Shards of glass are starting to peel away. The lighting is clinical, cold, and harsh, emphasizing the physical tension between the soft, organic-shaped glass and the rigid, unmarked metal. No text, no numbers. Shallow depth of field, tactile textures. 8k. --ar 16:9
visual_logic: A fragile glass bird is destroyed by the act of measurement. An unmarked steel caliper crushes the subject, proving that extreme pursuit of a metric can physically obliterate the system being quantified.
